purpose: Machine-readable Google ranking-update and policy/search-feature timeline for drift analysis. It supports temporal correlation only and must not be used to claim causation.
next_review: 2026-08-10 or immediately after an official Google ranking or policy update
reverification_note: 2026 policy/update entries independently re-verified against Google-owned sources on 2026-07-10; current through the June 24-26 2026 spam update. The unconfirmed June 19 2026 volatility is intentionally excluded.
source_precedence: third-party source only as quarantined context
usage_rules: Use rollout windows, not announcement headlines alone, when aligning traffic changes.
A temporal overlap is correlation, not proof that an update caused a change.
Separate ranking updates, service incidents, manual actions, documentation changes, and policy enforcement.
Do not encode third-party volatility labels, traffic-loss percentages, or unnamed studies as verified events.
Reconcile against the official status JSON/history before every client-facing update analysis.
If the official ranking history or incident record changes, reconcile the canonical file immediately rather than waiting for the routine review date.
Treat each event ID as unique. Do not duplicate the same rollout across ranking_updates, policy_and_feature_changes, or service_incidents.
Store no client URLs, traffic data, credentials, personal data, or confidential incident analysis in this public reference file.
Validate unique IDs, ISO dates, start/end ordering, approved source domains, and chronological ordering before release.
Consumers must preserve the event source and evidence state and must not silently promote quarantined claims.

ranking_updates.name: September 2023 helpful content update
ranking_updates.notes: Official helpful content update before the system was incorporated into broader core systems in 2024.

ranking_updates.name: March 2024 core update
ranking_updates.notes: Complex broad core update; Google also announced new spam policies on the start date.

ranking_updates.name: August 2024 core update
ranking_updates.notes: Official broad core update. A separate ranking incident also began on 2024-08-15; do not merge the incident with the update when analyzing traffic.

ranking_updates.name: December 2025 core update
ranking_updates.notes: Official broad core update. Do not attach third-party traffic-loss percentages or E-E-A-T claims without separate evidence.

ranking_updates.name: February 2026 Discover update
ranking_updates.notes: Official Discover update; do not treat it as a general web-search core update.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: FAQ rich results restricted
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: FAQ rich results were restricted primarily to well-known authoritative government and health sites.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: HowTo rich result removed
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: HowTo rich results were removed on desktop and mobile.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: Expired domain, scaled content, and site reputation abuse policies announced
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: Policy event, not a standalone ranking-update window. Correlate enforcement and manual actions separately.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: INP became a Core Web Vital
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: INP replaced FID as the stable responsiveness Core Web Vital.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: Mobile-first indexing migration completed
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: Google completed the migration to mobile-first indexing.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: Site reputation abuse policy clarified
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: Google clarified that first-party involvement or oversight does not change the third-party nature of content used to exploit host ranking signals; editorial wording was further clarified in January 2025.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: Lesser-used structured-data displays phased out
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: Course Info, ClaimReview, Estimated Salary, Learning Video, Special Announcement, and Vehicle Listing displays were phased out; Book Actions later required context-specific handling.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: Reporting and test support removed for phased-out structured-data displays
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: Search Console rich-result reporting and Rich Results Test support were removed for the named phased-out types.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: Search Quality Rater Guidelines edition
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: Guideline edition date. Rater scores do not directly determine an individual page ranking.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: Practice Problem structured-data documentation removed
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: Google stated that the Practice Problem feature is no longer shown in Search.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: Back button hijacking added to spam policies
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: Google announced the explicit policy on 2026-04-13 with enforcement beginning 2026-06-15; pages may face manual actions or automated demotions.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: Google generative-AI optimization guide published
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: Google states that generative Search remains SEO; no special AI markup, ideal length, or content chunking is required.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: FAQ rich-result documentation removed
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: Google stated the FAQ rich result is no longer shown in Search.

policy_and_feature_changes.name: Google clarified llms.txt is neither required nor a ranking lever
policy_and_feature_changes.notes: Part of the May 15 2026 generative-AI optimization guide. Google states machine-readable/AI text files including llms.txt are not used by Google Search and will not harm or help visibility. Independently re-verified 2026-07-10.

quarantined_claims.claim: A named update gave E-E-A-T a new numeric weight or targeted a specific business model without a Google statement.
quarantined_claims.action: Do not encode or use in causal reporting.

quarantined_claims.claim: A fixed percentage traffic loss is typical for a core or spam update.
quarantined_claims.action: Use the client data and documented rollout window; do not generalize a third-party sample.

quarantined_claims.claim: A third-party SERP volatility spike is itself an official Google update.
quarantined_claims.action: Keep as analyst context outside the canonical event list unless Google confirms it.

service_incidents.name: Ranking issue concurrent with August 2024 core update
service_incidents.notes: Analyze separately from the August 2024 core update; overlapping dates do not make them the same event.

failure_policy.official_history_unavailable: Return the last verified snapshot as stale context only; do not claim current completeness.
failure_policy.invalid_or_partial_date: Quarantine the entry; do not use it for rollout overlap calculations.
failure_policy.third_party_only_event: Keep outside ranking_updates and policy_and_feature_changes until a primary source confirms it.
failure_policy.conflicting_google_sources: Preserve both sources, mark the event blocked for causal reporting, and escalate for reconciliation.
